methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $125M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Mythic is a Series D chip design company that has raised $125M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 8 private companies and 3 public semiconductors (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) spanning frontier chip architectures and established players.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x multiplier to the $125M raised proxy, implying a $188M valuation. Peer-median analysis across 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—SambaNova Systems ($5.0B, Series D), Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C)—yields a median of $2.7B, with a range of $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable; the sector P/S median across semiconductors is 24.2x, but Mythic's revenue is not on file, preventing a meaningful cross-check. 

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $159M (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation for pre-revenue or early-revenue frontier chip companies. The stage-progression method produces an outlier low estimate; peer-median and its range dominate the framework. Key sensitivities include actual revenue run-rate and capital intensity relative to peers. The $529M–$5.0B peer band itself signals substantial dispersion in how the market values earlier-stage chip development.
